data.title: Trust Wallet Offers Fee-Free BNB Smart Chain Transactions for Limited Time
data.description: According to PANews, Trust Wallet has announced a promotional period from March 20, 2025, at 17:00 to March 27, 2025, at 16:00 (UTC+8), during which all token transactions on the BNB Smart Chain (BSC) will be exempt from transaction provider fees. This initiative is supported by 1inch and 0x. While users will still be responsible for network or gas fees, the transaction provider fees will be completely waived. This offer is exclusively available for transactions conducted through the Trust Wallet app's trading feature, and third-party dApp transactions are not eligible.

data.title: PancakeSwap Surpasses Major Protocols in Daily Revenue
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Defillama indicates that PancakeSwap's revenue reached $4.56 million in the past 24 hours. This achievement places it third on the cryptocurrency revenue leaderboard, surpassing notable protocols and blockchains such as Jupiter, Tron, Lido, and Uniswap.

Tether and Circle secured the top two positions with 24-hour revenues of $18.35 million and $6.16 million, respectively.

data.title: Trump Media Executives Eye Acquisition of U.S. Crypto Firm
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, three executives from Trump Media & Technology Group are leading a company that may seek to acquire a U.S.-based cryptocurrency or blockchain firm. This move aligns with the Trump administration's support for the sector.

The executives are aiming to secure a $179 million public and private offering through Renatus Tactical Acquisition Corp I, a special-purpose acquisition company (SPAC) based in the Cayman Islands. This information was revealed in a regulatory filing dated March 14, initially reported by Forbes. Eric Swider, CEO of Renatus Tactical, is also a director at Trump Media and previously served as CEO of Digital World Acquisition Corp., a SPAC that facilitated Trump Media's public listing. Alexander Cano, the operating chief of Renatus Tactical, was the president of Digital World, while Devin Nunes, CEO and chair of Trump Media, also chairs Renatus Tactical.

Renatus Tactical has not specified its acquisition targets but mentioned the possibility of acquiring "one or more businesses" and investing in data security and technology for both military and non-military applications. The company intends to focus on high-potential businesses within the United States, although it is open to opportunities in various industries worldwide.

The SPAC plans to raise over $178.94 million through the sale of 17.5 million public shares at $10 each and more than 3.94 million private placement warrants at $1 each. In its filing, Renatus Tactical highlighted U.S. President Donald Trump's administration's significant efforts to incorporate digital assets into the national financial strategy. This includes Trump's executive orders to establish a Bitcoin reserve and a crypto stockpile, as well as a directive for a working group to propose cryptocurrency regulations.

Despite these initiatives, the company acknowledged potential challenges due to its association with Trump and Trump Media. Some entities may hesitate to engage with Renatus Tactical because of its management team's and board of directors' affiliations with Trump. This issue has also affected Tesla, whose share price has dropped over 40% this year, partly due to CEO Elon Musk's role as a White House cost-cutting czar, leading to backlash and vandalism against Tesla properties across the U.S.

Trump holds a majority stake in Trump Media, which operates the social media platform Truth Social. Forbes estimates Trump's net worth at approximately $4.8 billion, while Bloomberg suggests it exceeds $6.5 billion. Both sources agree that his 114.75 million shares in Trump Media, valued at $2.36 billion at the current closing price of $20.59, constitute a significant portion of his wealth. Trump placed these shares in a trust in December to mitigate potential conflicts of interest before his inauguration.

data.title: Walrus Foundation, the entity behind the Walrus protocol, completes $140 million in financing, led by Standard Crypto
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Walrus Foundation, the entity behind the Walrus protocol, has completed $140 million in financing by selling its cryptocurrency $WAL. Standard Crypto led the investment, and other investors include a16z, Electric Capital and Franklin Templeton Digital Assets. The sale values ​​the total supply of the Walrus protocol cryptocurrency at $2 billion. The project will build a decentralized storage network. (Fortune)

data.title: Blockchain Investigator Links High-Leverage Trader to Convicted Fraudster
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, blockchain investigator ZachXBT has identified a high-leverage trader, who recently profited $20 million through leveraged positions, as potentially linked to William Parker. Parker, previously known as Alistair Packover, was arrested last year for stealing approximately $1 million from two casinos in 2023 and was sentenced in Finland. In the early 2010s, he was frequently in the UK news for fraud charges related to hacking and gambling.

Despite serving time for fraud, Parker appears to have continued his gambling activities. In January 2025, he reportedly used high leverage in online gambling, employing six-figure sums to achieve significant profits. This activity follows his history of exploiting casino games and phishing attacks.

data.title: CZ: The number of followers on X platform has exceeded 10 million. Interacting with others does not mean that you approve of them.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Binance Co-founder CZ posted on the X platform: "Cryptocurrency has grown and my fans have also grown, now exceeding 10 million. Thank you for your support during this journey. I try to interact with as many people as possible, but I can't do background checks on everyone. My interaction does not mean recognition. Stay SAFU."

data.title: The Monad testnet is currently suspended and is being restored
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Monad co-founder Keone Hon said that the Monad testnet is currently suspended due to the failure to reach majority consensus at the epoch boundary. This round of epoch was originally planned to add 15 validators to the active validator set, but the conversion process did not go as expected. The validator nodes are working hard to resolve the problem and resume network operation as soon as possible.

data.title: ZachXBT: The 50x leveraged whale on Hyperliquid may be a criminal who was arrested for casino theft
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News ZachXBT published an investigation on the identity of the 50x leveraged whale on Hyperliquid on the X platform. By tracking on-chain transactions, it was found that a person named "William Parker" was related to the 50x leveraged whale on Hyperliquid. Last year, he was arrested for stealing about $1 million from two casinos in 2023 and sentenced in Finland. Before that, he was called Alistair Packover (William Peckover), and later changed his name.
In the early 2010s, Alistair Packover made headlines in the UK several times for fraud charges related to hacking and gambling. It is clear that he has not learned his lesson after serving years for fraud and may continue to gamble. The funds are currently mainly stored in:
0x51d99A4022a55CAd07a3c958F0600d8bb0B39921

data.title: Gate.io Launches Bedrock(BR) Spot Trading and Starts HODLer Airdrop
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Gate.io will launch Bedrock (BR) spot trading at 21:00 on March 20, 2025 (UTC+8), and will start the HODLer Airdrop from 19:00 on March 20 to 18:00 on March 21 (UTC+8). Holders of 1 GT can receive the airdrop for free and share 960,000 BR tokens. The upper limit for individual participation is 1,000 GT.

data.title: Fox reporter: Ripple CEO’s net worth has reached about $10 billion, but the company lacks information disclosure
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Fox reporter Charles Gasparino posted on the X platform that as the price of XRP rises, Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse's personal net worth has reached about $10 billion, but Ripple does not seem to have publicly submitted any documents about how much money the company has made. After all, Ripple is a traditional enterprise, which will be a problem that Paul Atkins and the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission need to pay attention to in the future, because according to securities laws, these are usually required to disclose financial information.
